summary: Retest of Focus Mode challenge picker FAILED. E1 claimed to replace Alert.alert with a custom <Modal> containing 3 buttons, but the Modal JSX was NEVER added to focus.tsx render tree. Styles (pickerBackdrop, pickerSheet, pickerBtn, pickerCancel) and state (challengePicker, setChallengePicker) are defined, but the <Modal> element is completely absent from all three return blocks. Tapping Exit Early sets state=true but nothing renders. None of challenge-picker, challenge-pushups, challenge-breathing, challenge-cancel testIDs exist in DOM.

ui_bugs.issue: Modal JSX is missing from render tree. Modal is imported, challengePicker state exists, and picker* styles are defined, but no <Modal visible={challengePicker}>...</Modal> block was added to the JSX. Tapping focus-escape sets state but nothing visually appears. The claimed fix was not actually implemented in the component render.

integration_issues.flow: Focus Mode → Exit Early → Challenge Picker
integration_issues.issue: Flow is broken at the picker step. User cannot access pushups or breathing challenges at all on web. focus-start → timer counts down → focus-escape tap → NO modal appears. Downstream flows (pushup-tap, breath-orb) are unreachable.

react_native_issues.issue: Deprecation warnings in console: 'shadow*' style props are deprecated — use 'boxShadow'. props.pointerEvents deprecated — use style.pointerEvents. Non-blocking but worth fixing.

action_items:
E1 MUST add the actual <Modal> JSX block to focus.tsx main return (around line 184, before <View style={styles.closeRow}> or inside SafeAreaView). Use existing styles and state. Template:

<Modal visible={challengePicker} transparent animationType='fade' onRequestClose={() => setChallengePicker(false)}>
  <View testID='challenge-picker' style={styles.pickerBackdrop}>
    <View style={styles.pickerSheet}>
      <Text style={styles.pickerTitle}>Choose Your Challenge</Text>
      <Text style={styles.pickerDesc}>Complete a challenge to exit early.</Text>
      <TouchableOpacity testID='challenge-pushups' style={[styles.pickerBtn, {backgroundColor: colors.green}]} onPress={() => { setChallengePicker(false); setChallenge('pushups'); setPushupCount(0); setMode('challenge'); }}>
        <Text style={styles.pickerBtnText}>20 Push-ups</Text>
      </TouchableOpacity>
      <TouchableOpacity testID='challenge-breathing' style={[styles.pickerBtn, {backgroundColor: colors.cyan}]} onPress={() => { setChallengePicker(false); setChallenge('breathing'); setBreathCycle(0); setMode('challenge'); }}>
        <Text style={styles.pickerBtnText}>4 Breath Cycles</Text>
      </TouchableOpacity>
      <TouchableOpacity testID='challenge-cancel' style={[styles.pickerBtn, styles.pickerCancel]} onPress={() => setChallengePicker(false)}>
        <Text style={[styles.pickerBtnText, {color: colors.textSecondary}]}>Keep Focusing</Text>
      </TouchableOpacity>
    </View>
  </View>
</Modal>

After adding Modal, verify challenge handlers set mode='challenge' so the conditional rendering blocks for pushups/breathing trigger (currently they check challenge === 'pushups' which works independently of mode, but ensure state transitions are correct).
critical_code_review_comments: focus.tsx imports Modal from react-native but never renders it — unused import indicating incomplete fix.
challengePicker state is set to true via tryExit() but there is no consumer in JSX — dead state.
Replace shadow* style props with boxShadow for web compatibility (React Native Web deprecation).

success_rate.frontend: 0% - challenge picker flow completely broken, Modal never rendered

context_for_next_testing_agent: After E1 adds the <Modal> JSX, retest: (1) /focus loads, (2) focus-start begins timer, (3) focus-escape opens challenge-picker modal with 3 buttons, (4) challenge-pushups routes to pushups screen, tap pushup-tap 20 times completes, (5) challenge-breathing shows breath-orb animating 4 cycles (~24s per cycle = ~96s total, use long wait or reduce timing for test). Use mobile viewport 375x667.
rca_of_issue: Root cause: E1's fix was partial — they added the Modal import, state variable, and CSS styles for the picker, but forgot to add the actual <Modal>...</Modal> JSX block to the component's return statement. Reproduction: Navigate to /focus → tap Start Focus Session → tap Exit Early (Challenge) → nothing happens visually (only state changes internally). DOM scan confirms only focus-close, focus-timer, focus-escape testIDs exist. Mitigation: Add the Modal JSX block in the main return's SafeAreaView, wired to challengePicker state with the 3 TouchableOpacity buttons using existing styles.
